## [00:00 - 03:03]

Let's go All right, welcome back to the pubcast I'm John Loomer and today let's talk about look-alike audiences now This is a feature. It's been around since 2014. I know because I wrote about it in 2014 and back in March of 2014 and back then it made a lot of sense, right? So you may recall the targeting inputs we provided were super important We defined who would see our ads we would provide interests and maybe custom audiences I mean 2014 there weren't a ton of options then but it was really hard to know Who are the proper people that we should be putting in here especially with interests but by providing look-alike? Audiences now we could give meta. Okay. These are our ideal people our Website visitors our email list our paying customers our Facebook followers now go out find people like these people Right, so this allowed us to scale to expand our audience Find people beyond those who are already engaged with us We're already on our email list and find people who we assume are similar to them, right? There's a lot of voodoo going on there. We don't really know how it worked We kind of just took it for granted that it was a good way to create a much broader audience To reach new people but targeting works differently now. First of all, it's much smarter Second there's far less targeting control right whether you use broad targeting or not Your audience is automatically Expanded in many cases. So if you use advantage plus audience Whatever you provide is just a suggestion if you use original audiences But you optimize for a purchase or a conversion of some kind you look like audiences expanded your detail targeting is expanded Right, so it's all just kind of us giving the algorithm ideas potentially, but we really don't know how much that stuff matters I've done tests that suggest that meta pretty much ignores anything we put in there now if it can be expanded So really now targeting is built around the algorithm that searches for people who are willing to perform our desired action, so if you want a purchase it's gonna search out people who are likely to purchase from you and especially when it's a purchase the algorithm actually bakes in Remarketing and I've done this with my own testing you should test your own but using audience segments and sales campaigns I found that anywhere from 25 to 35 50% even of my budget is automatically spent on remarketing when I'm going broad and that's a big deal

## [03:03 - 06:06]

So in a way the algorithm was already working as one Big look-alike audience while also remarketing at the same time So while look-alike audiences make a lot of sense in theory they may have been Required ten years ago because it was so confusing Building that broader audience and knowing where to start that helped us because there are too many options Honestly when it came to interest and behaviors back then that we didn't really know which ones were valid Which ones weren't which ones were effective which ones were there's so much testing That had to be done to figure that out but now the algorithm does all that for us And even if you make the argument that algorithmic targeting has flaws which it does you can't really avoid it anymore Right, so when you use that look-alike audience, that's usually just kind of a suggestion to start It's going to expand off of that in many cases But the algorithm needed something like a look-alike audience ten years ago It needed us to define who this source was that it could find more people like that But the concept is outdated meta already knows who your most valuable audience is and meta prioritizes those people We know that is already happening that is proven with audience segments Now I won't ever say that something never works or you shouldn't ever use look-alike audiences But I gotta admit I'm pretty close in this case providing look-alike audiences in my opinion is Unnecessary busyness because you think it's necessary and you think what you're doing is productive and Making an impact, but I don't really believe it's doing anything now. You can prove this Don't just take my word for it run tests. I'm not saying just run one test at ten dollars a day or something This is something you've got to continually test and decide on your own whether this still makes sense So test running look-alike audiences like you normally do and first of all if you use look-alike audiences with advantage plus audience That's pretty much ignored anyway Just so you know it's probably not contributing to your results in any way fusing look-alike audiences with original audiences and it's expanded Just remember that so you're relying on the same algorithm to build look-alike audience that you trust It's that same network that's running algorithmic targeting for you to right understand that But anyway test it with original audiences look like audiences use advantage plus audience without suggestions at all It sounds scary, but do it especially when optimizing for purchases set it up that way the bottom line here You know find what works for you if you've proven to yourself over and over especially with large sample size and with budgets That this is still effective for you I'm not gonna tell you you're crazy and don't do it But oftentimes what you're doing is creating more work for yourself than is necessary Especially if you're creating multiple assets for your targeting one that's look like one that's something else one that's something else Etc etc etc, which is inefficient But keep questioning your assumptions because what I believe 10 years ago is no longer true to me
